rule: hit = (kw>=2) OR (sem>=0.7) OR (func>=0.7); any single hit triggers FAIL (not-novel)
candidate_summary: BATIK-WAX-RESIST null-space negative-mask fine-tune: learnable per-weight mask subnetwork m_T(W) produces 0/1 protection masks; only UNPROTECTED weights updated as W_T = W + (1 - m_T(W)) ⊙ Δθ_T; sequential multi-task layering preserves earlier tasks' protected regions. Combines (i) learnable mask subnetwork + (ii) negative-protect interpretation + (iii) sequential multi-task layering.

results_analysis.title: Boosting LLMs with Mask Fine-Tuning (MFT)
results_analysis.rationale: MFT learns and applies BINARY MASKS to LLM weights — exact twin of learnable per-weight mask subnetwork on LLMs.

results_analysis.title: Structured Sparsity Dual Lottery Tickets Continual Multi-task (LoTA/LoTTO)
results_analysis.rationale: LoTTO learns MUTUALLY SPARSE MASKS for sequential tasks to prevent overlap — identical to batik sequential negative-mask layering.

results_analysis.title: Multi-Stage LLM Fine-Tuning with Continual Learning
results_analysis.rationale: Covers sequential multi-task fine-tune discipline on LLMs.

results_analysis.title: GCL Group-shared Continual Learning for Sparse LLMs
results_analysis.rationale: GCL preserves mask positions across tasks — same multi-task sequential mask preservation.

results_analysis.title: Progressive Networks, PackNet, HAT modular architectures
results_analysis.rationale: PackNet and HAT use mask-based freezing to PROTECT previous task weights — exact negative-protect mechanism.

results_analysis.title: Scaling Sparse Fine-Tuning to LLMs
results_analysis.rationale: Sparse fine-tuning at LLM scale covers the mask-selection substrate.

results_analysis.title: Mask-tuning: Improving Pre-trained LM Generalization
results_analysis.rationale: Mask-tuning for LMs satisfies kw>=2, sem>=0.7, and func>=0.7.

results_analysis.title: Fine Art of Fine-Tuning Structured Review
results_analysis.rationale: Generic review; no specific construct match.

verdict_sentence: Candidate FAILS novelty: 7 of 8 results independently hit by keyword (>=2), semantic (>=0.7), and functional (>=0.7) criteria — MFT (2503.22764) is an exact twin of the learnable binary-mask construct on LLMs, LoTTO provides the sequential mutually-sparse mask multi-task layering, and PackNet/HAT supply the negative-protect mechanism; all three core components of BATIK-WAX-RESIST are already published.
